metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify and pin the two Claude Code hook-contract assertions (`CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` env-var name and the SessionStart stdin `session_id` field name) against a version-tagged snapshot of the hooks reference; add a comment in the script and/or a doc note citing the specific Claude Code version/date at which the contract was confirmed, so a future contract change is detectable.
findings.finding: The plan's entire injection mechanism rests on two unverified Claude Code CLI capability assertions: (1) that `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` is the correct env-var name for hook-to-session env injection, and (2) that the SessionStart stdin envelope uses the JSON field name `session_id`. Both are stated as 'Claude Code hook-contract facts' and sourced only from a live URL (https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks) with no version pin, no locally-vendored snapshot, and no inline verification — `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` appears zero times anywhere in the codebase (src, docs, tests, scripts, CLAUDE.md). The graceful-degradation design (silent exit 0 if `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` is unset) means a wrong env-var name is indistinguishable from 'hook not yet configured', making the shim silently inert for ALL local users with no diagnostic signal, which is the very failure mode the plan aims to fix.
findings.location: What section — Hook script description; 'the Claude Code hook-contract facts — the SessionStart stdin `session_id` field and the `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` env-injection file — per the Claude Code hooks reference (https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `docs/session-id-shims.md` section (or a script header comment) that quotes the relevant clause from the Claude Code hooks reference verbatim, tags it with the CLI version it was verified against (e.g. `claude-code >= 1.x`), and notes the field name and env-var name explicitly. Optionally vendor a minimal fixture JSON (`{"session_id": "..."}`) in `tests/unit/fixtures/` as a documented sample of the SessionStart payload shape, so the test suite encodes the assumption explicitly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend AC6 (or add a new AC) to assert that `docs/event-schema.md` and `session_id.py` also no longer contain the old `claude-code_<ver>` form and do contain the base-name vocabulary, so all three described vocabulary-alignment edits are verified.
findings.finding: The vocabulary-alignment edits to `docs/event-schema.md` and `session_id.py` are described in the What section and listed in Scope, but AC6 only asserts the change in `docs/config.md` (checks for removal of the old slash-form and presence of 'base name `claude-code`'). No AC verifies that the old `claude-code_<ver>` form is removed-and-replaced in `docs/event-schema.md` or that `session_id.py`'s comment is updated.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC6's proof assertion: also check that `docs/event-schema.md` does not contain the raw ``claude-code_<ver>`` literal and does contain 'base name `claude-code`' (or the equivalent updated text), and that `session_id.py` comment is updated similarly. This can be added to the same `test_docs_document_provenance` test or as a new parameterised assertion in `test_session_shims.py`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC3 sub-case (and matching test) verifying that when CLAUDE_ENV_FILE is set but the write fails (unwritable path / missing parent directory), the script still exits 0 and never blocks the session.
findings.finding: The test plan does not cover the case where CLAUDE_ENV_FILE is set to a path that cannot be written (e.g. a read-only file, a non-existent parent directory, or a directory itself). The script's 'NEVER blocks the session' guarantee requires exit 0 even on write failure, but no AC or test case verifies this, so a write error could cause a non-zero exit that blocks Claude Code startup — the exact failure mode the design exists to prevent.
findings.suggested_fix: In the script, wrap the append/write operation in a try/except (Python block) or redirect stderr and append `|| true` to the shell write command, then add a pytest parametrize case in `test_session_shims.py` that passes a read-only or non-existent-parent path and asserts returncode == 0.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` is the correct Claude Code hook env-injection variable by consulting the live Claude Code CLI release notes, `--help` output, or a pinned SDK reference, and record the evidence in the plan or a brief spike; if the mechanism differs, update the shim's injection approach accordingly.
findings.finding: `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` — the env var name the entire env-injection mechanism depends on — appears zero times in the repo codebase (src/, docs/, tests/) and the sole citation is an unverifiable training-era URL (`https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks`). If the actual Claude Code CLI uses a different mechanism or var name (e.g. no `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` at all, or a different JSON-sourcing mechanism), the shim silently no-ops for every user, defeating the plan's purpose. This is an unverified external capability the plan's main deliverable rests on.
findings.location: What section — Hook script description; AC2; AC3

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm the exact field name of the `session_id` in the Claude Code `SessionStart` stdin JSON envelope against a live source (e.g. running `claude` with a debug hook or reading the published SDK types), and record the verified field name in the plan; update the shim and AC2's mock stdin if the name differs.
findings.finding: The `session_id` field name in the SessionStart stdin JSON envelope is an unverified external API surface. The plan asserts the payload contains `{"session_id": "…"}`, but this specific field name is not confirmed by any fetchable source — the sole reference is the same unverifiable URL. If the field is named differently (e.g. `sessionId`, `id`, `hookData.session_id`), the python3 parse silently produces a missing/blank `session_id` and no-ops.
findings.location: What section — 'Reads the SessionStart JSON envelope from stdin (field `session_id`)'; AC2

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify the exact `settings.json` JSON key path for Claude Code hook registration (e.g. by checking a live `claude` installation's config schema or release notes), record the verified key path, and update the docs install snippet and AC5 assertion accordingly.
findings.finding: The `hooks.SessionStart` JSON key path used in `settings.json` for hook registration (referenced in AC5 and the docs install snippet) is part of the same unverified external Claude Code hooks API surface. If the actual config key is spelled differently (e.g. `Hooks`, `hooks.onSessionStart`, or a top-level array), the hook never fires and the shim is never invoked.

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a single canonical `AI_AGENT`/`claim_harness` vocabulary table (e.g. in `docs/config.md` or `session_id.py`) listing all valid base names and the optional-suffix rule, and ensure AC6 asserts all three files (config.md, event-schema.md, session_id.py) reference it consistently.
findings.finding: The plan introduces bare `claude-code` as a new valid `AI_AGENT`/`claim_harness` vocabulary value and makes `_<version>` optional, but the complete updated canonical vocabulary for this field is never defined in one place. The existing three reference points (docs/config.md:352, docs/event-schema.md:145, session_id.py:45 comment) all currently list the form `claude-code_<ver> / opencode / codex / cursor`. The schemas (ticket_state.schema.json, ticket_state_llm.schema.json) declare `claim_harness` / `chn` as opaque `string|null` with no enum, so the docs examples ARE the vocabulary contract. The plan says to update those examples but never specifies what the single authoritative updated vocabulary list looks like — bare `claude-code` is added, but whether `claude-code_<ver>` remains valid alongside it, and in what form the list should read across all three files, is left to the implementer.
findings.location: What > Vocabulary alignment; AC6; docs/config.md:352; docs/event-schema.md:145; src/rebar/_commands/session_id.py:45
findings.suggested_fix: In the What > Vocabulary alignment section, specify the complete updated vocabulary list explicitly: `claude-code` (bare base name, optional `_<version>` suffix), `opencode`, `codex`, `cursor` — and update AC6 to assert all three source files contain this same form (not just docs/config.md).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact line-matching predicate the strip step uses (e.g. `^export REBAR_SESSION_ID=` and `^export AI_AGENT=` as prefix anchors) in the hook script description, co-located with the idempotent re-fire protocol.
findings.finding: The idempotent strip-then-write processing protocol is described behaviorally ('STRIPS this hook's own prior REBAR_SESSION_ID/AI_AGENT exports … preserving other hooks' lines') but the exact predicate for which lines to strip is never specified. This is a decision rule co-location gap: the protocol must be precise enough for an implementer to write it without ambiguity, but the plan omits the matching rule (e.g. 'lines matching `^export REBAR_SESSION_ID=`', 'lines matching `^export AI_AGENT=`'). The 'preserving other hooks' lines' guarantee depends entirely on this predicate being correctly scoped.
findings.location: What > Hook script, idempotent re-fire paragraph
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the idempotent re-fire description: 'Strip is implemented by filtering lines that match `^export REBAR_SESSION_ID=` or `^export AI_AGENT=` (prefix-anchored, no partial-line matching).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at minimum a stderr warning (not an error exit) for distinguishable failure cases — specifically when python3 is not found or when CLAUDE_ENV_FILE is set but the write fails — so operators can detect misconfiguration without blocking the session.
findings.finding: Silent no-op on python3 absence or parse failure means a misconfigured environment (python3 not on PATH) produces no user-visible signal; the operator cannot distinguish 'shim ran and session_id was captured' from 'shim silently failed and provenance is broken'.
findings.location: What section: 'any parse failure, a missing/blank session_id, or an absent CLAUDE_ENV_FILE is a silent no-op with exit 0 (fire-and-forget; NEVER blocks the session)'
findings.suggested_fix: Emit `echo 'claude-code-session-start: WARNING: python3 not found, session provenance disabled' >&2` (or similar) for the python3-absent case, and a write-error warning if the file append fails, while still exiting 0.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that `python3` availability in the Claude Code hook execution environment is guaranteed by rebar's install contract, OR replace the justification with the actual guarantee (e.g., 'uses the same Python as rebar via `${REBAR_PYTHON:-python3}`') and add a fallback / detection note to the install docs.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `python3` is 'already a rebar dependency' to justify its use in the hook script, but `pyproject.toml`'s `requires-python >= 3.11` governs the rebar package installation environment — not the shell environment where Claude Code fires the `SessionStart` hook. The hook runs in the user's login shell context, where `python3` may be on `PATH` via a different mechanism (pyenv, conda, system package) or not at all. The two contexts are independent: rebar being installed with Python 3.11 does not guarantee `python3` is resolvable in the hook's execution environment.
findings.location: What section: 'Uses `python3` (already a rebar dependency) to parse the JSON robustly'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either update ticket 7656 to explicitly name `docs/session-id-shims.md` as the page to extend, OR add a placeholder section stub for Codex/Cursor in `docs/session-id-shims.md` itself so the shared-page contract is enforced at create-time.
findings.finding: The OUT-scope exclusion 'OUT: Codex/Cursor shims (S6 / 7656, which extend the same docs page)' asserts that ticket 7656 will extend `docs/session-id-shims.md`. Ticket 7656's description and `file_impact` say only 'per-harness install docs' and 'docs/' — the specific page `docs/session-id-shims.md` is never named. This creates a coordination gap: if 7656's executor creates a different docs file (e.g., `docs/codex-shim.md`), the shared install page this plan creates becomes a stub with no Codex/Cursor content and no cross-reference, and the `docs/session-id-shims.md` cross-link from `docs/config.md` covers only Claude Code.
findings.location: Scope section: 'OUT: Codex/Cursor shims (S6 / 7656, which extend the same docs page)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Qualify `session_id.py` with its full repo path `src/rebar/_commands/session_id.py` everywhere it appears in Scope and AC6.
findings.finding: `session_id.py` is referenced by bare filename in the Scope section without its full repo path, while the actual file is `src/rebar/_commands/session_id.py`. Although only one file with that name exists in the repo, the unqualified reference creates a locatability ambiguity for an implementer scanning the plan.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `session_id.py` with `src/rebar/_commands/session_id.py` in the Scope edit-target list and in AC6's description.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add either (a) a manual smoke-test step in `docs/session-id-shims.md` (e.g. 'run `claude` with the hook registered and verify `REBAR_SESSION_ID` is set') that the completing agent documents as executed, OR (b) an explicit note in the plan stating that live end-to-end validation requires a running Claude Code session and is deferred to the user acceptance step with rationale, so the absence is intentional and reviewers are not left guessing.
findings.finding: No AC exercises the end-to-end live hook-invocation path (Claude Code fires the hook → env-file is read by the CLI), and the plan contains no explicit rationale for deferring or omitting this end-to-end check.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1–AC6 (all proving commands run offline against the script directly)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Manual verification' subsection to AC5's doc assertion (or a standalone AC7) that describes the smoke-test command: start a Claude Code session with the hook registered, then `echo $REBAR_SESSION_ID` inside the session. Mark it as 'human-run / not CI-automatable' with an explicit rationale, satisfying E6(f)'s deferral-with-rationale escape hatch.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add atomic file update to the hook script (e.g., write to a temp file then `mv` atomically, or use `flock`) so that concurrent SessionStart invocations cannot interleave their strip-then-write cycles on CLAUDE_ENV_FILE.
findings.finding: The strip-then-write sequence used for idempotent re-fire is NOT atomic: two concurrent SessionStart invocations (e.g., from subagents, which the plan explicitly flags as a concern in G1) can interleave their read-strip-write cycles, corrupting CLAUDE_ENV_FILE or causing one session's exports to be silently dropped.
findings.location: What section — hook script description: 'Idempotent: re-firing on resume/clear/compact first STRIPS this hook's own prior REBAR_SESSION_ID/AI_AGENT exports from the env file … then re-writes the current values'
findings.suggested_fix: Use `flock -x CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` around the strip-and-rewrite block, or write to a temp file and `mv -f` it into place (atomic on POSIX), to eliminate the TOCTOU race.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define a retire/cleanup path for REBAR_SESSION_ID and AI_AGENT in CLAUDE_ENV_FILE — either via a paired SessionEnd hook that strips those lines, or by documenting that CLAUDE_ENV_FILE must be a temp file scoped to the Claude Code process lifetime.
findings.finding: There is no RETIRE/cleanup path for the REBAR_SESSION_ID and AI_AGENT exports written to CLAUDE_ENV_FILE: after the Claude Code session ends, these lines persist in the file and can be sourced by unrelated subsequent processes or sessions.
findings.location: What section — hook script description and Scope section
findings.suggested_fix: Add a paired SessionEnd hook that strips the REBAR_SESSION_ID and AI_AGENT lines, OR require in the install docs that CLAUDE_ENV_FILE point to a per-invocation temp file (e.g., created in a wrapper script) so it is automatically cleaned up when the session process exits.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief ADR or CLAUDE.md section capturing the hook-shim pattern decisions (CLAUDE_ENV_FILE contract, strip-then-write idempotency, AI_AGENT base-name convention, exit-0 no-op contract) so that S6/7656 and future shim authors have a canonical reference.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel shim pattern (hook-based env-file injection for session provenance) that will be replicated by S6/7656 for other harnesses, but no ADR or CLAUDE.md update is specified to capture the architectural decision, leaving future implementers without a canonical design reference.
findings.location: Scope section: 'OUT: Codex/Cursor shims (S6 / 7656, which extend the same docs page)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `CLAUDE.md` to the vocabulary-alignment scope (or explicitly call out that its stale chain description is a pre-existing doc debt excluded from this story's scope).
findings.finding: The vocabulary-alignment scope (What + Scope sections) lists `docs/config.md`, `docs/event-schema.md`, and `session_id.py` as the three files to update, but `CLAUDE.md` line 218 also documents the session-id precedence chain and currently omits `OPENCODE_SESSION_ID` — a stale description the plan does not address. If `CLAUDE.md` is agent-facing guidance (it is: 'rebar — agent guide'), this omission means agents reading `CLAUDE.md` get a different, stale picture of the resolver chain.
findings.location: What section: 'Vocabulary alignment. Update the `claude-code_<ver>` examples in `docs/config.md`, `docs/event-schema.md`, and the `session_id.py` comment'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `CLAUDE.md` line ~218 to the list of files updated in the Vocabulary alignment step, updating the parenthetical to include `OPENCODE_SESSION_ID`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the idempotent strip+rewrite of `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` uses an atomic write (write new content to a sibling temp file, then `mv`/`os.replace` it over the target) so the file is never transiently empty during the re-fire path.
findings.finding: The idempotent re-fire mechanism (strip prior exports, then append fresh ones) performs a non-atomic read-modify-write on CLAUDE_ENV_FILE: if any concurrent reader (e.g. another hook that sources the env file, or a Claude Code runtime that reads it during initialization) accesses the file between the truncation/strip step and the re-append step, it will see a transiently empty or partial file and miss REBAR_SESSION_ID / AI_AGENT for that window.
findings.location: What section — hook script description: 'first STRIPS this hook's own prior REBAR_SESSION_ID/AI_AGENT exports from the env file … then re-writes the current values'
findings.suggested_fix: In the hook script's Python snippet, build the new file content in-memory (filter out old REBAR_SESSION_ID/AI_AGENT lines, append fresh ones), write to `${CLAUDE_ENV_FILE}.tmp.$$`, then `mv "${CLAUDE_ENV_FILE}.tmp.$$" "${CLAUDE_ENV_FILE}"` — POSIX `mv` within the same filesystem is atomic. Update AC4's description to note atomic replacement as the required mechanism.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
